query: Kyushu Electric Power Transmission Distribution missing SSD customer data recovery status June 2026 九州電力送配電 SSD 紛失
answer:
This research yielded comprehensive results. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# Kyushu Electric Power Transmission & Distribution — SSD Customer Data Loss Incident (June 2026)

## Incident Overview

On **June 8, 2026**, Kyushu Electric Power Transmission and Distribution (九州電力送配電株式会社) publicly announced that a portable external SSD containing massive amounts of customer data had gone missing from a server room. The incident is one of the largest personal data exposure risks in Japan's utility sector.

## Timeline of Events

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| **April 27, 2026** | A contractor's employee completed a backup operation and placed the SSD in an **unlocked cabinet** inside a secure server room. |
| **May 26, 2026** | The SSD was discovered missing when the next scheduled backup was to be prepared — a **~30-day detection gap**. |
| **June 4, 2026** | A **theft report (被害届)** was filed with and accepted by the **Fukuoka Prefectural Police**. |
| **June 8, 2026** | Public announcement via press conference and press release. METI issued a formal order under the Electricity Business Act. |

## Scale of Affected Data

- **Up to 10.9 million customer accounts (約1,090万口)** — covering virtually all electricity customers across the Kyushu mainland (excluding remote islands).
- Data spanned contracts from **July 2016 through April 2026** (nearly a decade).
- Includes customers of both Kyushu Electric Power and other retail electricity providers in the Kyushu service area.

## Data Categories on the SSD

- Customer names (需要者名)
- Supply location addresses (供給場所住所)
- Electricity usage data (使用電力量データ)
- Phone numbers (電話番号)
- Retail electricity provider names (小売電気事業者名)

**Not included:** Bank account numbers, credit card information, or customer ages.

**Critically, the SSD had NO encryption and NO password protection.**

## Security Failures Identified

1. **No encryption** — The SSD was completely unencrypted, violating industry best practices.
2. **No password protection** — No access credential was set.
3. **Unlocked storage** — The cabinet within the server room was not locked.
4. **One-month detection gap** — No asset tracking or periodic inventory system existed.
5. **Contractor management gaps** — Insufficient tracking of media handled by contractor personnel.

## Recovery Status (as of mid-June 2026)

- **The SSD has NOT been recovered.**
- **No confirmed data leak or misuse** has been detected so far.
- Investigation is **ongoing** — both internal and via Fukuoka Prefectural Police.
- The company committed to **individually notifying all affected customers**.
- Recurrence prevention measures are **under development** but specifics have not yet been publicly announced.

## Government & Regulatory Response

- **METI (経済産業省)** issued an order on June 8, 2026 under **Article 106, Paragraph 3 of the Electricity Business Act**, requiring the company to report facts, identify root causes, and submit recurrence prevention measures.
- The **Personal Information Protection Commission (個人情報保護委員会)** was notified.

## Official Statements

- **Vice President Katsumi Inatsuki (稲月勝巳)** led a press conference on June 8, 2026 in Fukuoka, apologizing and pledging preventive measures.
- Official statement: *"We deeply apologize for causing great concern and inconvenience to our customers."*

## Key Sources

- Kyushu Electric Power T&D official press release (June 8, 2026)
- Sankei Shimbun, Yomiuri Shimbun, Mainichi Shimbun (June 8, 2026)
- METI press release on regulatory order (June 8, 2026)
- Nikkei xTECH security analysis
- FBS Fukuoka Broadcasting

key_facts: On June 8, 2026, Kyushu Electric Power Transmission & Distribution (九州電力送配電) announced a portable external SSD containing customer data had gone missing from a server room.
The SSD contained data on up to 10.9 million customer accounts (約1,090万口), covering virtually all electricity customers across the Kyushu mainland from July 2016 through April 2026.
Data on the SSD included: customer names, supply location addresses, electricity usage data, phone numbers, and retail electricity provider names. No bank or credit card data was included.
The SSD had NO encryption and NO password protection.
A contractor's employee completed a backup on April 27, 2026 and placed the SSD in an unlocked cabinet. The loss was not discovered until May 26, 2026 — a ~30-day detection gap.
A theft report (被害届) was filed with Fukuoka Prefectural Police on June 4, 2026.
METI issued a formal order under Article 106, Paragraph 3 of the Electricity Business Act on June 8, 2026, requiring root cause analysis and recurrence prevention measures.
As of mid-June 2026, the SSD has NOT been recovered, but no confirmed data leak or misuse has been detected.
The company pledged to individually notify all affected customers and develop recurrence prevention measures.
Vice President Katsumi Inatsuki (稲月勝巳) led a press conference on June 8, 2026 in Fukuoka apologizing for the incident.
